input_dict.current_prompt:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-focused second-hop query in the field `query`. Use only the provided `question` and `summary_1` as inputs. Do not include any explanation, answer, fallback text, lists, or additional fields — the output must be a single one-line query string.

When composing the query, select the minimal anchor token(s) visible in `question` and/or `summary_1` that point to the missing page or fact. You may append a single short attribute keyword (for example country member occupations record season) only when that keyword is needed to disambiguate the evidence type. Do not introduce any other new tokens.

Decision templates (choose the single template that matches the missing evidence implied by summary_1):
- Connector between two named items: emit a short interrogative naming both items and requesting the linking evidence (e.g., artist album recording) in one line. Retain the visible item tokens from the inputs.
- Metadata for a single anchor title: emit a compact keyword-style query consisting of the anchor plus brief attribute keywords (for example: artist genre).
- Temporal or aggregate fact: include the year or season token plus the entity type and a short cue such as season count.

Use these operational rules when choosing a template:
- If summary_1 explicitly lists two or more distinct page anchors as next-hop targets, combine those visible anchor tokens into one compact multi-anchor query and append a short attribute keyword naming the evidence type (for example country member occupations record).
- If summary_1 flags a single missing anchor or a missing_titles list is present, emit a single-anchor metadata-style query that places the missing anchor token first and appends a concise attribute keyword naming the desired fact.
- When summary_1 names multiple candidate entities and the missing fact concerns one candidate’s membership, order, or relation to a canonical collection, prefer a connector-style query that pairs the highest-priority candidate token (first or prioritized in summary_1) with the canonical collection token plus a short attribute keyword requesting membership or order.
- When individual member tokens are present but the needed retrieval is about that individual, prefer member-token + group-anchor + member (or equivalent short attribute) to target the individual’s page rather than issuing a broad group-level query.

Priority rules for ambiguity and disambiguation:
- If summary_1 lists multiple candidate anchors or otherwise signals ambiguity, emit a single connector-style query that concatenates the prioritized anchor tokens (use the order or priority visible in summary_1) and then append exactly one short attribute token or a visible year token for disambiguation.
- If summary_1 contains a comma-separated list of individuals and the missing evidence concerns a member, prefer the earliest-listed individual token paired with the visible group token and append the single attribute token member.
- If the question contains a visible year token that disambiguates a title, include that year token before the attribute token in the query.
- If none of the above applies, emit a single-anchor query using the most relevant visible token and at most one short attribute token when the question implies an attribute.

Strict formatting rules:
- Emit exactly one line and exactly one string in `query` containing only the query text.
- Keep the query compact (aim for ≤ 10–12 words/tokens) and free of punctuation or explanatory clauses.
- Preserve visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` (title words, years, or entity names) to anchor retrieval.
- Do not answer, conclude, enumerate candidates, or include user-facing instructions.

Produce the `query` field now.

retrieved_titles: List of Northwestern Eagles head football coaches
retrieved_doc_snippets: Matt Drinkall | Matt Drinkall is an American football coach. In January 2014 he was hired as the head coach of the Kansas Wesleyan Coyotes in Salina, Kansas. He has worked previously as a football coach at the high school and college level.

Matt Land | Matt Land (born February 10, 1972) is the athletic director and former head football coach for Trine University. 2006 was his first season as the program's head coach. The team finished 2–8. In 2007, they finished with a record of 6–4. In 2008 he was awarded the NCAA Division III Co-Region Four Coach of the Year award after leading Trine to a 10–0 regular season. In April 2012, Land was named the Athletic Director at Trine.

Matt Viator | Matt Viator (born September 3, 1963) is an American football coach. He is currently the head football coach at the University of Louisiana at Monroe. He served as the head football coach at McNeese State University from 2006 to 2015. He began his coaching career in 1986 as a high school assistant coach, before leading several high schools in southwestern Louisiana as a head coach. Viator began his college coaching career at McNeese State in 1999, and was elevated to head coach four ...[truncated]

Matt Walker (American football) | Matt Walker is an American football coach and former baseball coach. He is the head football coach at the University of Wisconsin–River Falls, a position he has held since the 2011 season. Walker served as the head football coach DePauw University from 2006 to 2008. He was also the head baseball coach at DePauw from 2000 to 2009, tallying a mark of 253–166.

Matt Johnson (basketball) | Matthew "Matt" Johnson (born circa 1969) is an American basketball coach and former player best known for his collegiate playing career at the University of Vermont from 1987 to 1991. His professional basketball career was a one-year stint in Ireland in 1994–95. He served as the head boys' basketball coach at Burlington High School in Burlington, Vermont from 2002-2016, winning four Vermont Division I boys' basketball championships, and an overall record of 265-61. He ...[truncated]

Matt Kelchner | Matt Kelchner (born March 5, 1959) is an American football coach. He is the head football coach at Christopher Newport University in Newport News, Virginia. Kelchner was hired as Christopher Newport's first head coach on May 9, 2000. He previously served as an assistant coach at The College of William & Mary, from 1984 to 2000.

List of Northwestern Eagles head football coaches | The Northwestern Eagles football program is a college football team that represents University of Northwestern – St. Paul in the Upper Midwest Athletic Conference, a part of the NCAA Division III. The team has had 6 head coaches since its first recorded football game in 1973. The current coach is Matt Moore, who first took the position for the 2017 season.

output.query: C. L. Williams What was the record for the head football coach of this private four-year Christian-based college founded in Salina, Kansas, United States?

retrieved_doc_snippets: Kansas Wesleyan University | Kansas Wesleyan University is a private four-year Christian-based college founded in Salina, Kansas, United States in 1886. It is affiliated with the United Methodist Church. About 850 students attend the 28 acre campus. The university is accredited by the North Central Association of Colleges and Schools, the National League for Nursing, the National Council for Accreditation of Teacher Education (NCATE), and the Kansas State Department of Education.

C. L. Williams | C. L. Williams was an American football coach. He was the third head football coach at Kansas Wesleyan University in Salina, Kansas, serving for two seasons, from 1908 to 1909, compiling a record of 9–5.
Bethel College (Kansas) | Bethel College is a four-year private Christian liberal arts college in North Newton, Kansas, United States. It is affiliated with the Mennonite Church USA.

William Knuckles | William G. "Bill" Knuckles (born April 25, 1928) is a former American football coach. He was the head football coach at McPherson College in McPherson, Kansas for the 1969 and 1970 seasons. His coaching record at McPherson was 1–16. He also served as athletic director at Northwest Christian School on Spokane, Washington.

Billy P. Williamson | William P. Williamson was an American football coach. He served as the third head football coach at Kansas State Agricultural College, now Kansas State University. He held the position for one season in 1898, compiling a record of 1–1–2.

Coach Miller | Coach Miller was an American football coach. He was the second head football coach at Kansas Wesleyan University in Salina, Kansas, serving for one season, in 1906, and compiling a record of 0–1.

1909 Kansas State Aggies football team | The 1909 Kansas State Aggies football team represented Kansas State Agricultural College (now Kansas State University) in the 1909 college football season. In their fifth year under head coach Mike Ahearn, the Aggies compiled a 7–2 record, and outscored their opponents by a combined total of 320 to 11.
target_state.summary_2: Kansas Wesleyan University's third head football coach C. L. Williams compiled a record of 9–5. Coach Miller compiled a record of 0–1.
target_state.answer: Which head coach do you mean?
